title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement davidrsch@gmail.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct

Community Impact Guidelines inspired Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing to rrlmgraph-bench
text: recommend create Git branch pull request (PR). Look GitHub Actions build status making changes. README contains badges continuous integration services used package. New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use air package apply styles, comment /style PR formatted automatically. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. Comment /document PR regenerate man/ NAMESPACE automatically. use testthat. Contributions test cases included easier accept. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md current development version header describing changes made followed GitHub username, links relevant issue(s)/PR(s).

title: Getting help with rrlmgraph-bench
text: Thanks using rrlmgraph-bench. filing issue, places explore pieces put together make process smooth possible. Start making minimal reproducible example using reprex package. haven’t heard used reprex , ’re treat! Seriously, reprex make R-question-asking endeavors easier (pretty insane ROI five ten minutes ’ll take learn ’s ). additional reprex pointers, check Get help! section tidyverse site. Armed reprex, next step figure ask. ’s question: start community.rstudio.com, /StackOverflow. people answer questions. ’s bug: ’re right place, file issue. ’re sure: let community help figure ! problem bug feature request, can easily return report . opening new issue, sure search issues pull requests make sure bug hasn’t reported /already fixed development version. default, search pre-populated :issue :open. can edit qualifiers (e.g. :pr, :closed) needed. example, ’d simply remove :open search issues repo, open closed. right place, need file issue, please review “File issues” paragraph tidyverse contributing guidelines. Thanks help!

title: rrlmgraph Benchmark Report
text: vignette presents reproducible benchmark comparing six retrieval strategies providing R coding context LLM: Results loaded inst/results/benchmark_results.rds, regenerated automatically every Monday (demand) run-benchmark CI workflow using GitHub Models (gpt-4o-mini).

title: rrlmgraph Benchmark Report
text: First 6 rows benchmark results.
code: results_path <- system.file(   "results", "benchmark_results.rds",   package = "rrlmgraphbench" ) if (!file.exists(results_path)) {   stop("benchmark_results.rds not found -- trigger the run-benchmark workflow.") } all_results <- readRDS(results_path) knitr::kable(   head(all_results[, c("task_id", "strategy", "trial", "score", "total_tokens")], 6),   caption = "First 6 rows of benchmark results." )

title: rrlmgraph Benchmark Report
text: Mean score, 95 % CI, token usage, hallucination rate per strategy.
code: stats <- compute_benchmark_statistics(all_results) #> Warning: ! n_trials = 1: inferential statistics require >= 2 trials. #> ℹ Returning point estimates only (no CI, t-tests, or Cohen's d). #> ℹ Rerun with n_trials >= 3 for full statistical output. knitr::kable(   stats$summary[, c(     "strategy", "n", "mean_score", "sd_score",     "ci_lo_95", "ci_hi_95", "mean_total_tokens", "hallucination_rate"   )],   digits = 3,   caption = "Mean score, 95 % CI, token usage, and hallucination rate per strategy." )

title: rrlmgraph Benchmark Report
code: # Base-R dot chart sorted by mean score summary_df <- stats$summary summary_df <- summary_df[order(summary_df$mean_score, decreasing = TRUE), ]  dotchart(   summary_df$mean_score,   labels = summary_df$strategy,   xlab   = "Mean score (0–1)",   main   = "Strategy performance",   pch    = 19,   col    = "steelblue" ) segments(   x0   = summary_df$ci_lo_95,   x1   = summary_df$ci_hi_95,   y0   = seq_len(nrow(summary_df)),   lwd  = 2,   col  = "steelblue" )

title: rrlmgraph Benchmark Report
text: TER measures score-per-token relative full_files baseline. TER > 1 means strategy achieves higher score per token consumed. Token Efficiency Ratio relative full_files baseline.
code: ter_df <- data.frame(   strategy = names(stats$ter),   TER      = round(stats$ter, 3) ) ter_df <- ter_df[!is.na(ter_df$TER), ] ter_df <- ter_df[order(ter_df$TER, decreasing = TRUE), ] knitr::kable(ter_df,   row.names = FALSE,   caption = "Token Efficiency Ratio relative to full_files baseline." )

title: rrlmgraph Benchmark Report
text: Fraction responses containing least one hallucination. Hallucination type breakdown (available):
code:
hall_df <- stats$summary[, c("strategy", "hallucination_rate")] hall_df$hallucination_rate <- round(hall_df$hallucination_rate, 3) hall_df <- hall_df[order(hall_df$hallucination_rate), ] knitr::kable(hall_df,   row.names = FALSE,   caption = "Fraction of responses containing at least one hallucination." ) if ("hallucination_details" %in% names(all_results)) {   details_flat <- unlist(strsplit(     all_results$hallucination_details[nzchar(all_results$hallucination_details)],     "; "   ))   if (length(details_flat) > 0) {     type_pattern <- regmatches(       details_flat,       regexpr("invented_function|invalid_argument|wrong_namespace", details_flat)     )     type_counts <- sort(table(type_pattern), decreasing = TRUE)     barplot(       type_counts,       main = "Hallucination types",       ylab = "Count",       col = c("tomato", "goldenrod", "steelblue"),       names.arg = c("invented
function", "invalid
argument", "wrong
namespace")[         match(names(type_counts), c("invented_function", "invalid_argument", "wrong_namespace"))       ]     )   } else {     message("No hallucinations detected in the loaded results.")   } } #> No hallucinations detected in the loaded results.

title: rrlmgraph Benchmark Report
text: Welch t-tests Bonferroni correction Cohen’s d.
code: pw <- stats$pairwise if (!is.null(pw) && nrow(pw) > 0) {   pw$sig <- ifelse(pw$p_bonferroni < 0.001, "***",     ifelse(pw$p_bonferroni < 0.01, "**",       ifelse(pw$p_bonferroni < 0.05, "*", "")     )   )   knitr::kable(     pw[, c(       "strategy_1", "strategy_2", "statistic",       "p_value_raw", "p_bonferroni", "cohens_d", "sig"     )],     digits = 4,     caption = "Pairwise Welch t-tests. * p<0.05; ** p<0.01; *** p<0.001 (Bonferroni)."   ) } else {   message("Pairwise tests not available (requires n_trials >= 2).") } #> Pairwise tests not available (requires n_trials >= 2).

title: rrlmgraph Benchmark Report
text: Mean score disaggregated fixture project.
code: if ("task_id" %in% names(all_results)) {   # Infer project from task_id (e.g. task_001_fm_mini_ds → mini_ds).   # Use regmatches() so unrecognised task_ids return NA rather than the   # original string (which would silently create a spurious project level).   m <- regmatches(     all_results$task_id,     regexpr("mini_ds|shiny|rpkg", all_results$task_id)   )   all_results$project <- ifelse(     grepl("mini_ds|shiny|rpkg", all_results$task_id),     m, NA_character_   )   proj_summary <- aggregate(     score ~ strategy + project,     data = all_results,     FUN  = mean   )   proj_wide <- reshape(     proj_summary,     idvar = "strategy",     timevar = "project",     direction = "wide"   )   names(proj_wide) <- gsub("score\\.", "", names(proj_wide))   knitr::kable(     proj_wide,     digits  = 3,     caption = "Mean score disaggregated by fixture project."   ) }

title: rrlmgraph Benchmark Report
text: multi-trial results available, plot shows whether score improves across successive trials (proxy benefit context).
code: if ("trial" %in% names(all_results)) {   trial_means <- aggregate(score ~ strategy + trial,     data = all_results, FUN = mean   )   strategies <- unique(trial_means$strategy)   cols <- rainbow(length(strategies))   plot(     range(trial_means$trial), c(0, 1),     type = "n",     xlab = "Trial", ylab = "Mean score",     main = "Score by trial"   )   for (i in seq_along(strategies)) {     sub <- trial_means[trial_means$strategy == strategies[i], ]     lines(sub$trial, sub$score, col = cols[i], lwd = 2, type = "b", pch = 19)   }   legend("bottomright", legend = strategies, col = cols, lwd = 2, cex = 0.8) }

title: Authors and Citation
text: Díaz D (2026). rrlmgraphbench: Benchmark Suite rrlmgraph. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/davidrsch/rrlmgraph-bench.
code: @Manual{,   title = {rrlmgraphbench: Benchmark Suite for rrlmgraph},   author = {David Díaz},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/davidrsch/rrlmgraph-bench}, }

title: Benchmark Suite for rrlmgraph
text: goal rrlmgraphbench provide objective, reproducible benchmark measures whether rrlmgraph context retrieval actually helps LLMs solve real R coding tasks — much, compared simpler baselines. runs standard set coding tasks six retrieval strategies, scores LLM response, reports mean score, 95 % confidence intervals, token efficiency, hallucination rates per strategy. Results regenerated automatically every Monday committed back repository GitHub Pages site always shows live data.

title: Benchmark Suite for rrlmgraph
text: can install development version rrlmgraphbench GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("davidrsch/rrlmgraph-bench")

title: Benchmark Suite for rrlmgraph
text: run_full_benchmark() evaluates six strategies across every task built-task suite using LLM choice. "github" provider uses GITHUB_PAT (automatically set GitHub Actions) — extra secret needed.
code: library(rrlmgraphbench)  # Full run: GitHub Models, 3 trials per task x strategy pair. results <- run_full_benchmark(   output_path  = "results/benchmark_results.rds",   n_trials     = 3L,   llm_provider = "github",   llm_model    = "gpt-4.1-mini" ) #> ── rrlmgraphbench ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Tasks   : 15  (5 × fix_method, 5 × new_feature, 5 × bug_diagnosis) #> ℹ Strategies: 6 #> ℹ Trials  : 3  per strategy × task #> ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ✔ [1/90]  rrlmgraph_tfidf  ×  task_001_fm_mini_ds  trial 1  (score 0.8, 1 234 tok, 2.3 s) #> ✔ [2/90]  rrlmgraph_tfidf  ×  task_001_fm_mini_ds  trial 2  (score 0.8, 1 198 tok, 2.1 s) #> ... #> ✔ [90/90] random_k  ×  task_015_doc_rpkg  trial 3  (score 0.4,  892 tok, 1.8 s) #> ✔ Results saved to results/benchmark_results.rds  (90 rows × 16 cols)  # Quick integration check without calling an LLM (returns dummy 0.5 scores). dry <- run_full_benchmark(   output_path = tempfile(fileext = ".rds"),   .dry_run    = TRUE ) nrow(dry) #> [1] 90

what: Example 2: Compute and inspect benchmark statistics
title: Benchmark Suite for rrlmgraph
text: compute_benchmark_statistics() aggregates per-trial scores summary table 95 % confidence intervals, Token Efficiency Ratio (TER), pairwise Welch t-tests Bonferroni correction Cohen’s d. pairwise statistical significance:
code: stats <- compute_benchmark_statistics(results)  # Per-strategy summary (ordered by mean score). stats$summary[order(-stats$summary$mean_score),               c("strategy", "n", "mean_score", "ci_lo_95", "ci_hi_95",                 "mean_total_tokens", "hallucination_rate")] #>          strategy  n mean_score ci_lo_95 ci_hi_95 mean_total_tokens hallucination_rate #>  rrlmgraph_tfidf 45      0.743    0.702    0.784             3 821               0.044 #> rrlmgraph_ollama 45      0.729    0.687    0.771            4 103               0.067 #>    bm25_retrieval 45      0.651    0.608    0.694             6 440               0.111 #>        full_files 45      0.638    0.594    0.682            18 274               0.156 #>          random_k 45      0.547    0.502    0.592             2 108               0.133 #>        no_context 45      0.423    0.378    0.468               187               0.200  # Token Efficiency Ratio: score per token relative to full_files. # TER > 1 means better score at lower token cost. stats$ter #>  rrlmgraph_tfidf rrlmgraph_ollama   bm25_retrieval         random_k       no_context #>            3.544            2.953            0.977            2.517               NA pw <- stats$pairwise pw[pw$p_bonferroni < 0.05,    c("strategy_1", "strategy_2", "cohens_d", "p_bonferroni", "sig")] #>         strategy_1    strategy_2 cohens_d p_bonferroni sig #>  rrlmgraph_tfidf    no_context    1.821       <0.001  *** #> rrlmgraph_ollama    no_context    1.643       <0.001  *** #>  rrlmgraph_tfidf    full_files    0.423        0.031    *

what: Example 3: Detect hallucinations in LLM responses
title: Benchmark Suite for rrlmgraph
text: count_hallucinations() inspects generated R code invented function names, invalid argument names, wrong-namespace calls. benchmark calls automatically; can also use audit LLM-generated snippet.
code: # Invented function and wrong package namespace in the same snippet. code <- '   df <- dplyr::filtrate(mtcars, cyl == 6)   # "filtrate" does not exist in dplyr   result <- xgboost::xgb_train(df)          # "xgb_train" is not exported by xgboost '  count_hallucinations(code) #> [[1]] #> [[1]]$type #> [1] "wrong_namespace" #> [[1]]$fn #> [1] "dplyr::filtrate" #> [[1]]$detail #> [1] "'filtrate' is not exported by the 'dplyr' package" #> #> [[2]] #> [[2]]$type #> [1] "wrong_namespace" #> [[2]]$fn #> [1] "xgboost::xgb_train" #> [[2]]$detail #> [1] "'xgb_train' is not exported by the 'xgboost' package"  # Pass a graph to also trust project-internal functions. g <- rrlmgraph::build_rrlm_graph("path/to/mypkg") count_hallucinations(code, graph = g)

title: Benchmark Suite for rrlmgraph
text: Benchmark Report — live results latest automated run Reference — full function documentation rrlmgraph — package benchmarked

what: Compute benchmark statistics from a full results data frame — compute_benchmark_statistics
title: Compute benchmark statistics from a full results data frame — compute_benchmark_statistics
text: Aggregates per-trial results produced run_full_benchmark() comprehensive report containing: Summary table (mean, SD, 95 \ hallucination rate) per strategy. Token Efficiency Ratio (TER) relative "full_files" baseline. Pairwise two-sample Welch t-tests Cohen's d Bonferroni-corrected p-values. Mean Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain (NDCG) relevance ranks available.

title: Compute benchmark statistics from a full results data frame — compute_benchmark_statistics
code: compute_benchmark_statistics(all_results)

title: Compute benchmark statistics from a full results data frame — compute_benchmark_statistics
text: all_results data.frame produced run_full_benchmark(). Required columns: strategy Character. Strategy label. score Numeric [0, 1]. Task score. total_tokens Integer. Total tokens consumed. hallucination_count Integer. Optional columns NDCG: rank, relevant.

title: Compute benchmark statistics from a full results data frame — compute_benchmark_statistics
text: list following elements: summary data.frame one row per strategy. ter Named numeric vector. TER values; NA baseline strategy. pairwise data.frame pairwise test results. ndcg Named numeric NULL rank data absent.

title: Compute benchmark statistics from a full results data frame — compute_benchmark_statistics
text: strategy fewer 30 observations, normality tested stats::shapiro.test().  p < 0.05, bootstrap 95 \ confidence intervals (5 000 resamples) used instead normal-approximation CI.

title: Compute benchmark statistics from a full results data frame — compute_benchmark_statistics
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ results <- run_full_benchmark("inst/tasks", "inst/projects",                               tempfile(fileext = ".rds")) stats   <- compute_benchmark_statistics(results) stats$summary } # }

what: Count hallucinations produced by an LLM in a generated code snippet — count_hallucinations
title: Count hallucinations produced by an LLM in a generated code snippet — count_hallucinations
text: Parses code using base::parse() walks resulting AST find: Invented functions – calls names appear neither rrlmgraph call-graph (graph) current R session via getAnywhere(). Invalid arguments – named arguments listed base::formals() target function (checked function can resolved session). Wrong-package namespace calls – pkg::fn() references fn actually export fn. Non-standard-evaluation column references (bare names inside dplyr verbs, data.table indexing, formula RHS) flagged; detector inspects calls whose first element symbol.

title: Count hallucinations produced by an LLM in a generated code snippet — count_hallucinations
code: count_hallucinations(code, graph = NULL)

title: Count hallucinations produced by an LLM in a generated code snippet — count_hallucinations
text: code Character(1).  R source code, returned LLM. graph rrlm_graph object built target project, NULL (default).  provided, function names present nodes graph trusted even loadable current session.

title: Count hallucinations produced by an LLM in a generated code snippet — count_hallucinations
text: named list; element represents one detected hallucination fields: type Character. One "invented_function", "invalid_argument", "wrong_namespace". fn Character. function name involved. detail Character. Human-readable explanation. Returns empty list hallucinations found.

title: Count hallucinations produced by an LLM in a generated code snippet — count_hallucinations
code: code <- "result <- xyzzy_nonexistent_fn(mtcars, foo = 1)" count_hallucinations(code) #> [[1]] #> [[1]]$type #> [1] "invented_function" #>  #> [[1]]$fn #> [1] "xyzzy_nonexistent_fn" #>  #> [[1]]$detail #> [1] "'xyzzy_nonexistent_fn' could not be found in the R session or the project graph." #>  #>

what: rrlmgraphbench: Benchmark Suite for rrlmgraph — rrlmgraphbench-package
title: rrlmgraphbench: Benchmark Suite for rrlmgraph — rrlmgraphbench-package
text: Provides fixture projects ground-truth datasets benchmarking rrlmgraph package. Fixtures range minimal data-science script project medium-sized Shiny application small R package, covering full spectrum project types supported rrlmgraph.

title: rrlmgraphbench: Benchmark Suite for rrlmgraph — rrlmgraphbench-package
text: Maintainer: David Díaz davidrsch@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder]

what: Run the full rrlmgraph benchmark — run_full_benchmark
title: Run the full rrlmgraph benchmark — run_full_benchmark
text: Evaluates six retrieval strategies across every task tasks_dir using n_trials independent trials , persists combined results output_path.

title: Run the full rrlmgraph benchmark — run_full_benchmark
code: run_full_benchmark(   tasks_dir = system.file("tasks", package = "rrlmgraphbench"),   projects_dir = system.file("projects", package = "rrlmgraphbench"),   output_path,   n_trials = 3L,   seed = 42L,   .dry_run = FALSE )

title: Run the full rrlmgraph benchmark — run_full_benchmark
text: tasks_dir Path directory containing task JSON files (default: system.file("tasks", package = "rrlmgraphbench")). projects_dir Path directory containing benchmark project source trees (default: system.file("projects", package = "rrlmgraphbench")). output_path File path resulting data.frame saved RDS file.  Parent directories created needed. n_trials Integer(1). Number independent trials per task x strategy pair.  Defaults 3L. seed Integer(1). Random seed passed base::set.seed() stochastic operations.  Defaults 42L. .dry_run Logical(1). TRUE LLM called; dummy scores 0.5 returned.  Useful integration tests.

title: Run the full rrlmgraph benchmark — run_full_benchmark
text: data.frame (saved output_path also returned invisibly) one row per trial, containing columns: task_id Character. strategy Character. trial Integer. score Numeric [0, 1]. context_tokens Integer. response_tokens Integer. total_tokens Integer. latency_sec Numeric. hallucination_count Integer. hallucination_details List column (character vectors). syntax_valid Logical. runs_without_error Logical.

title: Run the full rrlmgraph benchmark — run_full_benchmark
text: LLM calls issued ellmer::parallel_chat() concurrency.  progress message emitted task x strategy combination together rolling time estimate.

title: Run the full rrlmgraph benchmark — run_full_benchmark
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ results <- run_full_benchmark(   output_path = "inst/results/benchmark_results.rds",   n_trials    = 3L,   seed        = 42L ) head(results) } # }

title: rrlmgraphbench 0.1.0
text: First release.

title: rrlmgraphbench 0.1.0
text: run-benchmark.yml CI workflow: added models: read permission GITHUB_TOKEN can call GitHub Models inference API. Without every ellmer::chat_github() call returned empty string scores degenerate 0.6 (#2). Removed [skip ci] auto-commit message pkgdown rebuilds benchmark report vignette every results update. LLM call failures run_single() now emit message() (warning()) visible CI logs.

title: rrlmgraphbench 0.1.0
text: Task corpus: 15 coding tasks across three fixture projects (mini data-science script, medium Shiny app, small R package), covering function-modification, bug-diagnosis, new-feature, refactoring, documentation categories. Ground-truth solutions committed inst/ground_truth/solutions/. run_full_benchmark() — evaluate six retrieval strategies every task. compute_benchmark_statistics() — summary table, TER, pairwise Welch t-tests, Cohen’s d, Bonferroni correction, NDCG. count_hallucinations() — detect invented functions, invalid arguments, wrong-namespace references LLM-generated R code. Vignette: benchmark_report — reproducible benchmark report using precomputed results.
